messages.content: What is the traditional Chinese word for "artificial"?
The traditional Chinese word for "artificial" is 人工 (rén gōng).
"computer/calculator"
The traditional Chinese word for "computer" or "calculator" is 計算機 (jì suàn jī).
"learning"
The traditional Chinese word for "learning" is 學習 (xué xí).
first person singular
The traditional Chinese word for "first person singular" is 我 (wǒ).
"love"
The traditional Chinese word for "love" is 愛 (ài).
"you"
The traditional Chinese word for "you" when addressing one person is 你 (nǐ).
"Stars" as in the big balls of nuclear plasma like our Sun.
The traditional Chinese word for "stars" as in celestial bodies like our Sun is 星星 (xīng xīng).
plural of suns/stars
The plural form of "suns" or "stars" in traditional Chinese is 星星們 (xīng xīng men).
"moving"
The traditional Chinese word for "moving" is 移動 (yí dòng).
What do the Chinese call prehistoric mammals like Adalatherium, Repenomamus, etc.?

The Chinese term for prehistoric mammals, such as Adalatherium and Repenomamus, varies depending on the specific species. Chinese scientific literature typically uses a combination of the Latin names and Chinese transliterations. For example, Adalatherium would likely be referred to as 阿达拉兽 (ā dá lā shòu) and Repenomamus as 热比娜兽 (rè bǐ nà shòu).
third person singular present tense verb

The traditional Chinese word for a third person singular present tense verb depends on the specific verb you are referring to. Different verbs may have different ways of conjugation. However, in general, verbs do not change their form for different persons or tenses in Chinese. The same verb form is used regardless of the subject or time.
"there is"
The traditional Chinese phrase for "there is" or "there are" is 有 (yǒu). It is commonly used to indicate the existence or presence of something.
singular form of "voice"
The traditional Chinese word for "voice" in singular form is 声音 (shēng yīn).
"in"
The traditional Chinese word for "in" is 在 (zài). It is commonly used to indicate location or being inside a certain place.
outer space/space/cosmos/universe

The traditional Chinese word for "outer space", "space", "cosmos", or "universe" is 宇宙 (yǔ zhòu). It refers to the vast expanse beyond the Earth that encompasses all celestial bodies, galaxies, and other cosmic phenomena.
"come"
The traditional Chinese word for "come" is 來 (lái). It is commonly used to indicate the action of approaching or arriving at a place.
"from"
The traditional Chinese word for "from" is 從 (cóng). It is commonly used to indicate the origin or starting point of something.
genitive form of sound

In traditional Chinese, the genitive form is not specifically used to indicate possession or attribution like in some other languages. However, when referring to the genitive form of "sound" in a construction like "sound of something," you can use the phrase 的聲音 (de shēng yīn). For example, "sound of the wind" would be 風的聲音 (fēng de shēng yīn) in traditional Chinese.
